item.title: MOTORING & ACCESSORIES | Arvin Kumar navigates tough times with Determination
item.description: FOR 30 years, the familiar sight of Arvin Kumar and his carrier trucks have been part of daily life at the Nabua carrier stand. At 56, Kumar has built a reputation for reliability, hard work and dedication, growing his small operation from a single carrier into a fleet of five.
item.content:encoded:
FOR 30 years, the familiar sight of Arvin Kumar and his carrier trucks have been part of daily life at the Nabua carrier stand.

At 56, Kumar has built a reputation for reliability, hard work and dedication, growing his small operation from a single carrier into a fleet of five.

Yet despite his success, he says making a living has become harder than ever.

Living in Vatuwaqa, Suva, Kumar owns and operates Na Lequ Ohe Carriers, a business that has served families and businesses across Suva and beyond for three decades.

Whether transporting household goods, construction materials or helping customers move to new homes, Kumar and his team are always ready to hit the road.

“My journey started with just one carrier,” Kumar said with pride.

“Through years of hard work and sacrifice, I now have five carriers operating from Nabua.”

But owning five trucks does not guarantee financial security. Rising fuel prices, fewer customers and the increasing cost of living have placed enormous pressure on small transport businesses like his.

Every weekday begins long before the city wakes. Kumar starts his day at 6am, preparing himself and his vehicles before arriving at the carrier stand by 7.30am.

His workday often ends around 5.30pm, but some days there is little work to show for the long hours.

“From Mondays to Fridays, business has become very slow,” he explained.

“There are days when we don’t get a single job. Customers ask about our prices, but many walk away because they think the cost is too high.

He says many people fail to realise that transport operators are also struggling with soaring expenses. “The price of diesel keeps increasing. We have to cover fuel, vehicle maintenance and other costs just to keep operating. It has become very difficult.

Despite the challenges, Kumar remains passionate about the profession that has supported his family for decades.

One of his greatest achievements is seeing his son follow in his footsteps.

“My son is now driving with me,” he said.

“I’m proud because he has seen how hard I’ve worked over the years.

I hope this motivates him to build a better future for his own family.”

Looking back, Kumar recalls a time when life felt much more affordable.

“Years ago, fuel prices were lower, groceries were cheaper and business was much better,” he said.

“People had more money to spend, and there was always work.”

Today, however, he believes families across Fiji are facing the same daily struggle.

“Everything has become expensive. Everyone is working harder just to survive and provide for their loved ones.”

Beyond business, Kumar is deeply concerned about the future of young people.

He says the growing presence of drugs and other social problems should serve as a warning for the next generation.

“My message to young people is simple stay focused on your goals, listen to your parents and work hard,” he said.

“Life today is not as easy as it was before. Success comes from discipline, honesty and determination.

After three decades behind the wheel, Arvin Kumar continues to show up each morning with hope, proving that resilience is measured not by the number of vehicles a person owns, but by the determination to keep moving forward, no matter how difficult the road ahead may be.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: PROPERTY GUIDE | Homes of super soccer stars
item.description: When it comes to the world’s highest-paid athletes, footballers are in a league of their own. The world’s most popular sport is also one of its most lucrative, with elite players earning staggering sums through club contracts, sponsorships, and endorsement deals. Stars like Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi have built fortunes that extend far beyond the pitch, transforming themselves into global
item.content:encoded:
When it comes to the world’s highest-paid athletes, footballers are in a league of their own.

The world’s most popular sport is also one of its most lucrative, with elite players earning staggering sums through club contracts, sponsorships, and endorsement deals. Stars like Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi have built fortunes that extend far beyond the pitch, transforming themselves into global brands worth hundreds of millions.

Unlike many American athletes, who often put down permanent roots in places like Los Angeles or South Florida, top footballers tend to accumulate homes throughout their careers. As they move between Europe’s biggest clubs—and increasingly to the Middle East—their real estate portfolios often grow alongside their reputations. The result is a line-up of lavish properties spanning Madrid, Barcelona, Dubai, Paris, and beyond.

Now, with the highly anticipated 2026 FIFA World Cup just around the corner, attention is once again turning to the sport’s biggest stars. Before they take the field, we’re taking a look inside the extraordinary homes they’ve amassed around the globe.

Kylian Mbappé’s Madrid house. Picture: Google Earth

Harry Kane’s Surrey house Picture: David Cannon

Neymar’s Mangaratiba house Picture: Google Maps

Lionel Messi’s Barcelona hous. Picture: Google Earth

Cristiano Ronaldo’s mansion, Cascais, Portugal Picture: Horacio Villalobos/Corbis

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Keep meals fresher | With refrigeration you can trust
item.description: FRESH food brings families together. Chilled drinks make every gathering more enjoyable. A reliable refrigerator keeps groceries fresher for longer and helps every kitchen run smoothly. At Narhari, you’ll find a complete refrigeration range from Berklays, Panasonic, Modyl, and Aiwa, giving every home the perfect balance of style, performance, and everyday convenience.
item.content:encoded:
FRESH food brings families together. Chilled drinks make every gathering more enjoyable. A reliable refrigerator keeps groceries fresher for longer and helps every kitchen run smoothly.

At Narhari, you’ll find a complete refrigeration range from Berklays, Panasonic, Modyl, and Aiwa, giving every home the perfect balance of style, performance, and everyday convenience.

The Berklays 183L Single Door Refrigerator with Water Dispenser is an excellent choice for smaller homes, apartments, offices, or anyone looking for practical cooling without taking up too much space.

Its built-in water dispenser provides refreshing cold water without opening the door, helping keep the inside temperature stable while adding everyday convenience.

Compact on the outside and surprisingly spacious on the inside, it delivers reliable performance that fits comfortably into modern living.

Panasonic continues to set the standard with innovative refrigeration technology designed to keep food fresher, healthier, and full of flavor.

The 202L Top Mount Refrigerator is ideal for smaller households, offering efficient cooling, practical storage, and dependable everyday performance.

Moving up in size, the 306L Top Mount Refrigerator provides additional space while incorporating intelligent energy-saving technology and advanced freshness features that help preserve fruits, vegetables, and groceries longer.

Larger families will appreciate the 332L Bottom Mount Refrigerator, thoughtfully designed with the freezer below for easier access to fresh food every day.

The 377L Bottom Mount Refrigerator with Water Dispenser combines generous storage with the convenience of chilled water on demand, making it perfect for busy households.

Premium models including the 500L Prime Refrigerator, 500L Prime Refrigerator with Water Dispenser, 503L Multi Door Refrigerator, and 618L Quad Door Refrigerator offer advanced food preservation technologies, flexible storage compartments, intelligent energy efficiency, and modern styling that transforms any kitchen into a beautiful living space.

The Modyl Refrigerator Range delivers dependable refrigeration at exceptional value.

Available in practical capacities that suit first homes, growing families, and rental properties, Modyl refrigerators combine efficient cooling, generous storage, and simple operation into appliances built for everyday living.

Clean designs, durable construction, and energy-conscious performance make Modyl a smart choice for homes that want reliability without compromise.

Aiwa Refrigerators blend contemporary styling with practical functionality.

From compact models ideal for smaller kitchens to spacious family refrigerators offering flexible shelving and organized storage, Aiwa makes daily food storage simple and convenient.

Attractive finishes, energy-efficient performance, and thoughtfully designed interiors help keep groceries organized while complementing today’s modern homes.

Every refrigerator in the Narhari collection is selected to meet different lifestyles, kitchen sizes, and household needs.

Whether you are furnishing a new home, upgrading your kitchen, or replacing an ageing refrigerator, there is a model designed to match your everyday routine while delivering dependable performance for years to come.

Step into your nearest Narhari showroom and discover the refrigerator that keeps your food fresher, your kitchen organized, and your family ready for every meal.

Available at Narhari stores, Suva, Lautoka, Nadi, Ba, Yadua-Sigatoka, Rakiraki, Nausori, Navua, Savusavu, Nakasi, Labasa.

Explore our full range, https://www.narharifiji.com

Stay connected for the latest deals, promos & new arrivals, facebook: https://www.facebook.com/narhari.fiji, Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/narhari.fj, TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@narhari_officialaccount.

Find the perfect fridge for your home, only at Narhari.

Keep food fresh and your kitchen stylish with Modyl refrigerators.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Unlock your child’s potential beyond health
item.description: WHEN people think about healthy eating for children, they often focus on physical growth. While good nutrition certainly helps children grow taller and stronger, its benefits extend far beyond height and weight. A balanced diet also supports brain development, improves concentration, strengthens emotional well-being, and helps children perform better both in school and in everyday life.
item.content:encoded:
WHEN people think about healthy eating for children, they often focus on physical growth. While good nutrition certainly helps children grow taller and stronger, its benefits extend far beyond height and weight.

A balanced diet also supports brain development, improves concentration, strengthens emotional well-being, and helps children perform better both in school and in everyday life.

The foods children eat every day directly affect how they think, learn, and feel. Nutritious meals provide the energy and essential nutrients needed for developing brains and growing bodies.

At St. Andrews Green Valley, healthy eating is more than just providing nutritious meals it is about creating lifelong habits that empower children to reach their full potential.

Nutrition is often described as the body’s fuel, but it is also the brain’s fuel.

Every lesson learned, every memory formed, and every challenge solved depends on a steady supply of nutrients.

Without proper nutrition, children may struggle to focus, experience low energy, or find it difficult to manage their emotions.

A healthy, balanced diet supports better concentration and memory, improved academic performance, strong physical growth and development, higher energy levels, better emotional regulation, long-term healthy eating habits

By making nutritious food a daily priority, parents can help their children build a strong foundation for lifelong success.

Healthy Eating Boosts Academic Performance and Concentration

The brain is one of the body’s most energy-demanding organs. It requires a continuous supply of nutrients to function efficiently.

Several essential nutrients play particularly important roles in supporting learning, memory, and focus.

Omega-3 fatty acids, particularly DHA, EPA, and ALA, are vital for healthy brain development.

DHA forms a significant portion of brain cell membranes, helping neurons communicate more effectively. This improves memory, concentration, and learning ability.

Excellent sources include, Fatty fish such as salmon and sardines, walnuts, chia seeds, flaxseeds.

B vitamins help convert food into usable energy for the brain. They also assist in producing neurotransmitters that regulate attention, mood, and cognitive function. Children who receive enough B vitamins are more likely to stay mentally alert throughout the school day.

Good sources include leafy green vegetables, eggs, bananas, whole grains, lean meats

Protein provides amino acids that the brain uses to produce important neurotransmitters such as dopamine and serotonin. These chemicals influence motivation, focus, mood, and emotional balance.

Healthy protein sources include, eggs, lean meats, fish, dairy products, beans, tofu. nuts

Together, these nutrients help children remain attentive in class, improve problem-solving skills, and support overall academic success.

Healthy Eating Supports Physical Growth and Development

Growing children require a wide variety of nutrients to build strong bones, muscles, and immune systems.

A balanced diet ensures they have the energy to stay active while supporting healthy development throughout childhood.

Calcium is essential for building strong bones and teeth. Since childhood is a critical period for bone growth, adequate calcium intake helps establish a healthy skeletal foundation that lasts into adulthood.

Rich sources include, milk, cheese, yogurt, leafy green vegetables.

Vitamin D works together with calcium by helping the body absorb it efficiently.

It also supports muscle function and strengthens the immune system, helping children stay healthy and active.

Sources include, fatty fish, egg yolks, fortified milk and dairy products, safe exposure to sunlight

Iron carries oxygen throughout the body, supplying muscles and the brain with the oxygen needed to produce energy.

Iron deficiency can lead to tiredness, poor concentration, and slower cognitive development.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Complete your home entertainment experience
item.description: AS anticipation builds for the FIFA World Cup Final, there has never been a better time to transform the living room into the ultimate match-day destination. In today’s fast-paced world, home is more than just a place to live it is where families gather, memories are made, and unforgettable sporting moments are shared together.
item.content:encoded:
AS anticipation builds for the FIFA World Cup Final, there has never been a better time to transform the living room into the ultimate match-day destination.

In today’s fast-paced world, home is more than just a place to live it is where families gather, memories are made, and unforgettable sporting moments are shared together.

Creating the perfect viewing experience starts with exceptional comfort. Overseas Lounge recliners are thoughtfully engineered with ergonomic design to provide outstanding support and relaxation, making every minute of the match as enjoyable as the last.

Whether you’re celebrating a spectacular goal, analysing every replay, or enjoying the post-match celebrations, these recliners deliver the comfort needed for every football fan.

Complementing this premium seating experience is a wide selection of upholstery options, including full leather, semi-leather, microfiber, polyester, and fabric finishes.

Designed to suit a variety of lifestyles and interior styles, each material combines sophisticated aesthetics with durability and easy maintenance, ensuring the lounge remains as practical as it is stylish.

The smooth manual recliner mechanism allows each family member to find their ideal seating position with ease, while generous seating space ensures everyone has the best seat in the house.

Modern styling and quality craftsmanship make Overseas Lounge recliners the perfect centrepiece for contemporary Fijian homes.

To complete the ultimate home entertainment experience, pair your Overseas Lounge recliner with a Hisense television.

Renowned for brilliant picture quality, vibrant colours, crystal-clear detail, and immersive sound, Hisense TVs bring every tackle, save, and spectacular goal to life.

Every pass across the pitch feels sharper, every crowd celebration more exciting, and every moment of the FIFA World Cup Final becomes an unforgettable experience.

Together, Overseas Lounge and Hisense create the perfect match combining premium comfort with world-class entertainment.

Whether you’re hosting friends, cheering alongside family, or simply enjoying football’s biggest night from the comfort of your own home, you’ll experience the FIFA World Cup Final exactly as it deserves to be seen.

This football season, bring the stadium atmosphere home. Relax in the comfort of an Overseas Lounge recliner, experience every thrilling moment on a Hisense television, and create memories that will last long after the final whistle.

With some of the best credit deals in town, Courts makes it easy for families across Fiji to enjoy the latest in home comfort and technology without stretching their budgets. Flexible payment options open the door for more households to experience the quality and comfort of modern overseas lounges to elevate their everyday living.

Scan the QR code for quick updates on OB and red pen deal locations and the latest red pen deals through our social media channels, Courts has also launch it’s my Courts app.

The app is built to simplify the customer experience by bringing shopping, account access, and customer support together in one easy-to-use platform. Customers can also explore current promotions, featured products, and exclusive offers directly from their mobile device.

The smooth manual recliner mechanism allows each family member to find their ideal seating position with ease.

Overseas Lounge recliners are thoughtfully engineered with ergonomic design to provide outstanding support and relaxation.

Right: Hisense create the perfect match, combining premium comfort with world-class entertainment.

Scan the QR code to download the myCourts app.

Scan the QR code to download the myCourts app.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Say goodbye to stains | Omo has you covered
item.description: LIFE in Fiji is beautiful. It is about vibrant communities, stunning beaches, and the irrepressible energy of family. But with that life also comes laundry. From the inevitable deep-seated food stains during family feasts to the dirt stains acquired from rugby matches, our clothes tell the story.
item.content:encoded:
LIFE in Fiji is beautiful. It is about vibrant communities, stunning beaches, and the irrepressible energy of family.

But with that life also comes laundry. From the inevitable deep-seated food stains during family feasts to the dirt stains acquired from rugby matches, our clothes tell the story.

These are stories we sometimes struggle to erase without fading the vibrant fabrics we cherish. It’s time to simplify. It’s time to discover the OMO difference.

​As many of us reflect on sustainability, choosing the right products for our homes becomes even more crucial.

This presents a powerful answer, showcasing the three pillars of a cleaning revolution.

This isn’t just about cleaning clothes; it’s about reclaiming your time and ensuring your family’s clothes are treated with the respect they deserve.

​Laundry is a chore we often dread. In many Fijian homes, we have all soaked clothes overnight, only to find the stain still mocking us.

Or we’ve used detergents so harsh they fade our favourite Sulu or wash-and-wear fabrics after just a few cleans.

We need a solution that is powerful, efficient, and gentle.

​OMO presents a modern answer! These aren’t old formulas in new packaging. They are re-engineered solutions, each with a unique purpose, ready to integrate seamlessly into your life.

Whether you are a dedicated hand-washer looking to soak laundry in soap powder before you scrub or love the convenience of utilising liquid detergents in washing machines – OMO has you covered.

​At the heart of the OMO ethos is a profound respect for your fabrics and your family.

In Fiji, where bright colours and delicate textiles are cherished, this is non-negotiable. Omo’s advanced formulas use selective dirt-targeting technology.

They are designed to attack dirt and grime at the molecular level without attacking the threads and dyes that give your clothing its life.

​This means you can say goodbye to faded dalo prints and rough, abrasive shirts. Your favourite outfits stay newer, longer.

The gentle touch of OMO ensures that even the softest infant garments are handled with care, making it the perfect choice for multi-generational homes.

When you choose OMO, you are investing in the longevity of your wardrobe.

​Choosing OMO is more than a household purchase; it’s a conscious decision for your family and our environment.

It’s choosing to spend less time scrubbing and more time making memories. It’s embracing innovation that delivers spectacular results without compromise.

Look for the distinctive red OMO packaging in stores across Fiji today.

Simplify your life. Experience the future of cleaning.

​OMO is a product of Unilever and is primarily distributed in Fiji by Motibhai Group of Companies.

Taking care of the family, one load at a time. Pictures: SUPPLIED

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Seeds of resilience
item.description: Bula Vinaka shoppers, Sometimes the smallest things can make the biggest difference. During my visits to the market days recently, I noticed several vendors selling seeds and seedlings. There were kumquat seedlings, chilli plants, curry leaves, baigani, dhania, cucumber seeds and plenty more. It reminded me that there is really no excuse for anyone wanting to start a small backyard garden. Some se
item.content:encoded:
Bula Vinaka shoppers, Sometimes the smallest things can make the biggest difference. During my visits to the market days recently, I noticed several vendors selling seeds and seedlings.

There were kumquat seedlings, chilli plants, curry leaves, baigani, dhania, cucumber seeds and plenty more. It reminded me that there is really no excuse for anyone wanting to start a small backyard garden. Some seed packets cost as little as $1, making them one of the cheapest investments a family can make.

A handful of vegetable seeds may not seem like much. They fit neatly into a small packet, yet in the right hands they can feed a family, reduce the weekly grocery bill and even provide a little extra income if extras are sold.

That is why this week’s donation of seeds worth $10,000 by UN Women to the Ministry of Agriculture, deserves attention. The seeds will support home gardening initiatives aimed at strengthening household food security and building community resilience across Fiji.

It may appear to be a modest donation, but it carries a much bigger message about self-reliance and the importance of growing some of the food we eat.

At the handover ceremony in Raiwaqa, Permanent Secretary for Agriculture Dr Andrew Tukana said the initiative was about more than simply providing planting materials.

“It is about nurturing resilience, strengthening self-reliance and cultivating hope for many households,” he said.

His message could not be timelier. Over the past few years, Fijians have faced one challenge after another.

The pandemic disrupted livelihoods, cyclones and floods damaged farms and homes, while rising global prices pushed up the cost of food, fuel and everyday essentials.

Many families continue to feel the strain every time they shop for groceries. Growing food at home offers a practical way to ease that burden.

A few rows of tomatoes, beans, cabbage, pumpkins, eggplant, bele or rourou can supplement family meals, cut shopping costs and provide fresh, nutritious food right outside the back door.

For many of our parents and grandparents, this was simply a way of life. Families grew tavioka, dalo, vegetables and fruit because that was how households fed themselves.

Buying every meal from the supermarket was never the norm.

UN Women Fiji Multi-Country Office representative Alison Miriam Davidian reminded us of that tradition when she said growing food at home had been passed down through generations of families living close to the land.

Perhaps it is a tradition worth embracing again. Modern lifestyles have made many of us increasingly dependent on supermarkets and imported food.

Yet Fiji is blessed with fertile land, a favourable climate and generations of farming knowledge.

Reconnecting with those strengths makes economic sense and builds healthier communities.

Home gardens also prepare us for difficult times.

After cyclones or severe flooding, families with established gardens often recover faster because they already have access to fresh produce while supply chains are being restored.

The seed funding, made possible through the Markets for Change programme with support from Australia, is a reminder that partnerships can help communities become more resilient.

But the real measure of success will not be the number of seed packets distributed. It will be the gardens that flourish, the children who eat healthier meals, the families who save money on groceries and the communities that become more self-sufficient.

Sustainable agriculture is about growing crops, ensuring families have food on the table, helping people become more self-reliant and protecting the land that future generations will inherit.

As Fiji continues to face the impacts of climate change, from stronger storms to coastal erosion, building resilience is no longer a choice.

Every home garden, every fruit tree planted and every sustainable farming practice is an investment in our future.

The choices we make today will shape the Fiji we leave behind. By growing even, a small portion of our own food, we are doing more than filling our plates.

We are building stronger, healthier and more resilient communities. Perhaps it all starts with a simple packet of seeds.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Young mum makes every dollar count
item.description: FOR 27-year-old Tuidali Raisele, grocery shopping isn’t simply about filling the pantry it’s about making thoughtful decisions that provide the best value for her family while maintaining a healthy and balanced lifestyle. Originally from Drudrua in Macuata, with European heritage, Tuidali now lives in Vatuwaqa, where she is raising her young child.
item.content:encoded:
FOR 27-year-old Tuidali Raisele, grocery shopping isn’t simply about filling the pantry it’s about making thoughtful decisions that provide the best value for her family while maintaining a healthy and balanced lifestyle.

Originally from Drudrua in Macuata, with European heritage, Tuidali now lives in Vatuwaqa, where she is raising her young child.

As a proud mother to a single child, she understands the importance of budgeting wisely while ensuring her family has everything they need.

Unlike many shoppers who remain loyal to one supermarket, Tuidali takes a different approach.

She believes that shopping around is one of the smartest ways to stretch the household budget, especially as the cost of living continues to climb.

“We don’t really have one set supermarket that we shop at,” she explains.

“We usually shop at Extra Supermarket in Laucala Beach, Shop N Save in Nabua, New World at Damodar City Centre and Cost-U-Less. It really depends on where we can find the best value and deals at the time.

Every Sunday evening, Tuidali heads out for her family’s main grocery shop, spending around $200 on essential household items.

Rather than making impulse purchases, she shops with purpose, carefully comparing prices and looking for specials that help maximise every dollar.

Her shopping trolley reflects a practical yet balanced lifestyle. It is typically filled with meat, fresh vegetables, fruits, toiletries, cleaning supplies and of course, a few treats for the family.

“I always make a conscious effort to include fresh fruits and vegetables because I’m a believer in having a balanced diet.

“For me, it’s not about being overly strict with food, but about making sure there is a good balance.”

That philosophy extends beyond the supermarket aisles. Tuidali is also a regular visitor to roadside stalls and the Suva Municipal Market, where she spends about $50 on fresh root crops, seasonal fruits and locally grown vegetables.

Shopping at the markets not only helps reduce household expenses but also allows her to enjoy fresh, quality produce while supporting local farmers and vendors.

It has become an important part of her family’s weekly routine and a practical way of keeping healthy foods on the table.

Like many Fijian families, Tuidali has had to adjust her spending habits as food prices and fuel costs continue to rise.

One of the biggest changes has been reducing meals eaten outside the home and embracing home cooking instead.

“With the rising cost of living, we’ve definitely had to become more mindful of how we spend,” she says.

“One of the biggest changes we’ve made is cutting down on eating out and choosing to make more things at home instead. It can take a little more effort, but it does make a difference to the household budget.”

For Tuidali, preparing meals at home isn’t just about saving money it also gives her greater control over what her family eats, making it easier to serve nutritious, balanced meals.

Her practical approach to shopping has taught her that a little planning can go a long way. Comparing prices, shopping seasonal specials and mixing supermarket visits with market shopping all contribute to keeping the family budget under control without sacrificing quality.

She encourages other shoppers to adopt the same mindset.

“My advice would be to always keep an eye out for the best deals and don’t be afraid to shop around,” she says.

“In this economy, good deals on basic food items can make a real difference, so when they come around, it’s worth taking advantage of them.”

She also believes every shopping trip is an opportunity to make healthier choices.

“I think it’s important to be intentional about what goes into your shopping basket. Creating healthier shopping habits can sometimes take a little more thought and effort, but even small choices like adding more fruits and vegetables or choosing to cook more meals at home can make a positive difference for you and your family.

For Tuidali Raisele, smart shopping is about more than finding bargains it’s about creating a healthier future, managing money wisely and proving that with careful planning, every dollar can go further while keeping family wellbeing at the heart of every purchase.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: MOTORING & ACCESSORIES | Minivan owner faces tough times
item.description: MOTORING & ACCESSORIES | Minivan owner faces tough times
